reason: The item cleanly tests protocol-bounded inference about early-warning windows and energy partition during thermal runaway, with correct choices requiring disciplined scope control (ARC vs other abuse heating; heat-to-cell vs ejecta). Decoys are largely single-fault and engineering-plausible, but some phrasing (e.g., absolute claims like “entirely” and “equivalent regardless”) creates mild cueing and reduces neutrality, keeping discriminative strength below S-tier.

The item cleanly targets a single, boundary-aware judgment: spotting the engineer’s unjustified leap from “SOC is primary” to “trigger effects are mere uncertainty,” then reconciling that with the comparable (or larger) observed trigger-method spread versus SOC-range trend. It has strong discriminative power because an expert must explicitly compare effect magnitudes and avoid both overgeneralization and hand-waving uncertainty claims, though the lack of explicit measurement-uncertainty bounds slightly softens D1/D4 by leaving “uncertainty” as an implicit, unquantified foil.
